So the big idea is really just an interesting story plus a unique mechanism plus one primary promise. That's what makes up the big idea. And so I break it down like this. The interesting story can be a personal story. It can be like a cover up story as you see in a lot of like financial and health stuff can be a terrifying story. So like in a lot of self-defense stuff, there's some type of like scary, horrifying story. You can have a story about their enemy. You see this in a lot of financial stuff where they're going after people like Hillary Clinton and stuff like that. You can have an embarrassing story. Stefan's group did really well on this with like their lift factor. Very embarrassing story about the woman. Shameful story. All those kind of stories work. The big, big, big thing here is that it has to be an emotionally interesting story. Just a plain old regular story that doesn't grip and pull them in is never going to work. So like someone was actually asking me about the lift factor offer that Stefan's team put out and asked me why I thought I'd convert it so well. And I was like, number one, it is probably one of the top five stories I've ever read in a sales letter. That's why if you took that story out, that offer would not work anywhere. Let's see. So this is like a classic John Carlton direct response ad for the people have never seen it. Let's break this one down. The story and this goes back to having a story that's an interesting story. A golfer who can just hit the ball 50 yards further than someone else is not interesting. A guy who's got one leg and can hit it farther than everybody is super interesting. That's really what makes this work. So this the story here as I've written down is literally the one leg of golfers hitting the ball further and straighter than anyone this guy had ever seen. That's a compelling story. The promise. You can see it right here. It's not magic at all. You see what I learned had almost nothing to do with me and everything to do with the amazing new swing he developed over the years. The interesting and compelling story that makes it really, it just makes it really freaking interesting is what you need. You can't just have a ho hum story. The difference between copy that works on the back end and copy that works on the front end is a really interesting story. You'll see a lot of stuff that works on the back end is very just feature and benefit driven. There's no big idea. You can write big like I said, if you're writing back on stuff, big idea, not really that necessary. If you just want to crank something out and make sales, but if you want something to work on the front end, it needs to have a big idea.

## [33:31 - 36:31]

You usually probably have one or two emotions that are kind of the core ones that your story touches on, but if you can make them feel four or five things, that's when you're really hitting home runs. I want to make sure two things. So I replied to Tanner in the chat, but Tanner did ask about the huge promise and why did it work so well. And what I put in there just worth pointing out is in this case, it is an instant wrinkle remover, so it really does actually work that way. It's like the wrinkles go away forever, but if you put it on within like a couple minutes, the wrinkles are gone and for several hours, it looks like you don't have wrinkles. So it's a very effective product in that regard. And I think as much of a huge process as it is, especially in beauty and skin care, women are often looking for those sort of holy grail, magic bullet type solutions.
